message:
Last month our power went out for about six hours on a random Tuesday afternoon, and honestly? My kids thought it was the best thing ever.

Instead of screen time, we set up our battery-powered lanterns in the living room like we were camping indoors, played card games, and I actually *talked* to my family without notifications pinging every thirty seconds. We kept our phones charged with a power bank I'd honestly forgotten we owned, and I made sure the fridge stayed mostly closed so our food would be okay.

The funny part is, nobody was stressed. We'd done a tiny bit of prep beforehand—nothing extreme, just keeping some basics on hand—so there was zero scrambling. It turned what could've been a frustrating situation into something kind of... nice?

I'm not saying I *want* power outages (please, no), but that day made me realize that being a little bit ready doesn't have to feel like doom-and-gloom planning. It just means you get to stay calm and maybe even enjoy your people instead of panicking.

Have you ever had a "silver lining" moment during something inconvenient like that?
